title: Pseudopyxis heterophylla
taxonomy: Pseudopyxis heterophylla is a species of flowering plant in the family Gentianaceae. It is native to China. The classification of Pseudopyxis heterophylla is as follows: it belongs to the kingdom Plantae, the clade Angiosperms, the clade Eudicots, the clade Asterids, the order Gentianales, the family Gentianaceae, the genus Pseudopyxis, and the species P. heterophylla. This species is characterized by its varied leaf morphology, which is reflected in its species name, heterophylla. Pseudopyxis heterophylla is an endemic plant species found in the mountainous regions of southern China.

intro: Pseudopyxis heterophylla, also known as Korean dodder or Pseudopyxis, is a perennial vine in the family Gentianaceae. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal issues. Bioactive constituents of Pseudopyxis heterophylla include flavonoids, alkaloids, and glycosides, which contribute to its pharmacological effects. Traditionally, the plant has been used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to relieve pain and inflammation.
